perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Affiliate" means any distributor or franchisee of HSWI or any company or other entity worldwide, including without limitation corporations, partnerships, joint ventures, and limited liability companies in which HSWI directly or indirectly holds at least a 19% ownership, equity, control or financial interest and which HSWI intends to enjoy any of HSWI's rights hereunder. A list of Affiliates as of the Effective Date is attached hereto as Attachment A. Attachment A may be modified from time to time upon notice by HSWI to reflect a revised list of Affiliates and each modified Attachment A shall be deemed incorporated herein by reference.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Affiliate" means any distributor or franchisee of HSWI or any company or other entity worldwide, including without limitation corporations, partnerships, joint ventures, and limited liability companies in which HSWI directly or indirectly holds at least a 5% ownership, equity, control or financial interest and which HSWI intends to enjoy any of HSWI's rights hereunder. A list of Affiliates as of the Effective Date is attached hereto as Attachment A. Attachment A may be modified from time to time upon notice by HSWI to reflect a revised list of Affiliates and each modified Attachment A shall be deemed incorporated herein by reference.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliate' is modified from requiring a 19% ownership stake to a 5% ownership stake. This creates an internal contradiction because it broadens the scope of what qualifies as an 'Affiliate.' Later in the document, specifically in Section 9.2, the term 'Affiliates' is used in the context of indemnification, where HSWI protects its Affiliates. Broadening the definition of affiliate significantly increases the scope of the indemnity, potentially creating financial exposure that wasn't originally intended under the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2 World Book. World Book hereby agrees to defend, indemnify and hold harmless HSWI, its Affiliates, and their officers, directors, agents, employees, advertisers, service providers, end users and related entities from all costs, damages, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable attorneys fees) arising out of: (a) any third party claim or action alleging that the Content, Affinities and Images (excluding any World Book Derivatives independently created by or on behalf of HSWI) in the manner delivered by World Book or as contemplated for HSWI's use hereunder infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property Rights of any third party; and/or (b) any third party claim or action arising out of World Book's material breach of this Agreement; provided that: (i) HSWI notifies World Book promptly in writing of any such threat, claim, and/or proceedings related thereto; (ii) World Book is given the opportunity to assume sole control over the defense and all related settlement negotiations, except that HSWI shall have sole authority over approval of any settlement that admits fault or liability on the part of HSWI; and, (iii) HSWI provides World Book with all reasonably necessary assistance, information and authority to perform the foregoing at World Book's expense.
perturbation.justification: The modification lowers the ownership stake required to be considered an 'Affiliate' from 19% to 5%. Section 9.2 provides indemnification to HSWI's 'Affiliates.' By broadening the definition of 'Affiliate,' the indemnity obligation is substantially increased, creating a direct and impactful contradiction in the financial exposure assumed by World Book. The changed text directly increases the scope of the indemnity obligation outlined in the contradicted text, creating a structural ambiguity, which according to the Perturbation Definitions, constitutes in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 License Grant and Restrictions. (i) License Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, World Book hereby grants to HSWI, a perpetual, irrevocable limited license to use, copy, store, archive, distribute, transmit, modify (subject to Section 2.1(iv)), and Display the Content, Images and Affinities in whole or in part, only on the HSWI Websites or in promotions in any media for the HSWI Websites.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 License Grant and Restrictions. (i) License Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, World Book hereby grants to HSWI, a temporary, revocable limited license to use, copy, store, archive, distribute, transmit, modify (subject to Section 2.1(iv)), and Display the Content, Images and Affinities in whole or in part, only on the HSWI Websites or in promotions in any media for the HSWI Websites.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the 'perpetual, irrevocable' license to 'temporary, revocable'. This creates an in-text contradiction when considering Section 6.1(ii), which states that "certain license grants for Content, Images and Affinities extend beyond the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement as set forth herein." Changing the license to 'temporary, revocable' directly contradicts the implication that some licenses are intended to outlast the agreement itself, creating ambiguity and uncertainty on which conditions or sections should be considered valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Term. (i) Term of Agreement. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, except as set forth in Subsection (ii), continue in full force and effect through the Delivery Period. (ii) Term of Licenses. Notwithstanding Subsection (i), certain license grants for Content, Images and Affinities extend beyond the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement as set forth herein. With respect to such Content, Images and Affinities, the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall continue to apply.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes the license from 'perpetual, irrevocable' to 'temporary, revocable'. This directly contradicts section 6.1(ii), which states that 'certain license grants for Content, Images and Affinities extend beyond the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement'. A temporary, revocable license cannot extend beyond the agreement's termination, creating a clear in-text contradiction and ambiguity regarding the license's duration and terms.

perturbation.original_text: 1.26 "Significant Breach" means an uncured breach (pursuant to the cure provisions of Section 6.2(i)) by HSWI of one or more of the following: (i) HSWI brands the Affinities with the word "Encyclopedia" and/or Displays the Affinities or the underlying Reference Content and/or Images as an Encyclopedia in breach of Section 2.2(iii); (ii) HSWI uses or Displays the World Book Brand to promote the Content in violation of Section 7.4; (iii) HSWI assigns this Agreement to a Competitor (as defined in Section 11.8) of World Book in violation of Section 11.8; and/or (iv) HSWI fails to make payments as required by Section 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.26 "Significant Breach" means an uncured breach (pursuant to the cure provisions of Section 6.2(i)) by HSWI of one or more of the following: (i) HSWI brands the Affinities with the word "Encyclopedia" and/or Displays the Affinities or the underlying Reference Content and/or Images as an Encyclopedia in breach of Section 2.2(iii); (ii) HSWI uses or Displays the World Book Brand to promote the Content in violation of Section 7.4; (iii) HSWI assigns this Agreement to a Competitor (as defined in Section 11.8) of World Book in violation of Section 11.8; and/or (iv) HSWI fails to provide World Book with a list of Key Staff as required by Section 4; and/or (v) HSWI modifies the delivery format as required by Schedule C.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing HSWI failing to provide a list of key staff and modifying the delivery format as significant breaches, this creates an in-text contradiction with Section 6.4, which states, 'For purposes of clarification, upon a Significant Breach of this Agreement by HSWI which remains uncured, the rights to publicly use the World Book Brand as set forth in Section 7.4 may be revoked by World Book.' Since providing key staff and delivery format are minor details, revoking the rights to use the World Book Brand can be considered a harsh and irrational response. This contradiction affects how Section 6.4 is interpreted and its enforceability, since the definition of significant breach may be contestable in court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.4 Remedies. The termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason shall not affect either party's rights or obligations arising prior to the effective date of such termination. For purposes of clarification, upon a Significant Breach of this Agreement by HSWI which remains uncured, the rights to publicly use the World Book Brand as set forth in Section 7.4 may be revoked by World Book. Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, the rights and remedies of the parties as set forth in this Agreement are not exclusive and are in addition to any other rights and remedies available to it at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces 'HSWI failing to provide World Book with a list of Key Staff as required by Section 4; and/or (v) HSWI modifies the delivery format as required by Schedule C' as a 'Significant Breach.' Section 6.4 states that upon a Significant Breach, 'the rights to publicly use the World Book Brand as set forth in Section 7.4 may be revoked by World Book.' The added breaches are relatively minor administrative issues, and revoking brand usage rights is a disproportionate and unreasonable penalty. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the penalty clause (6.4) is triggered by trivial offenses, undermining the reasonable and balanced application of contract terms. Therefore, ambiguity -- in text contradiction exists.
